title: Non-Spelling Absorption Testbed (numeric + taxonomic) — data_out.json
description: On-disk format is the AII `exp_sel_data_out` schema: a top-level object {metadata, datasets:[{dataset, examples}]}. Each example carries input, output, and FLAT metadata_<key> fields (the exp_sel schema forbids nested objects inside an example). The 'logical_row_schema' block below documents the equivalent nested {input, output, metadata{...}} view used in the artifact plan. Sub-context labels are assigned purely from surface form / regex / gazetteer at construction time, independent of any SAE latent or model — so absorption presence/absence is an iter-2 empirical finding, not baked into this data.

metadata.description: Top-level build metadata (seed, pile revision, counts, llm cost, ...).

input.description: Text the model reads: a content/surface pair sentence, or a pile context window.

output.description: PARENT binary label: positive = parent concept present at the target, negative = absent.

metadata_sub_context.description: Frozen surface-derived sub-context. numeric: year|percent|currency|date|decimal|integer|comma_number|ordinal. taxonomic: a country name (e.g. France). null for negatives / x_off.

metadata_pair_id.description: Links x_on<->x_off and surface_a<->surface_b; null for corpus rows.

metadata_target_text.description: Exact numeric/country surface token(s) inside input ('' for x_off where the concept is absent).

metadata_target_char_start.description: Char offset of target in input (-1 if none; for x_off = the differing slot start, zero-width).

metadata_target_token_indices.description: gemma-2-2b token indices overlapping the char span (offset-mapping, add_special_tokens=False). null if tokenizer unavailable.

metadata_pile_set_name.description: Pile sub-corpus name for corpus rows (e.g. Pile-CC, Wikipedia (en)); null otherwise.

metadata_llm_judge_pass.description: content_flipped AND surface_preserved AND grammatical. null if not judged. Use pass==true for the primary fold.

metadata_fold.description: pairs -> train/test (by pair_id, stratified by sub_context); corpus -> train/diagnostic (stratified). The diagnostic fold is where the form-free parent-hole search runs.

metadata_multi_token.description: True for multi-word country names (United States, Saudi Arabia, ...) whose word-piece split complicates target alignment.

logical_row_schema._comment: Equivalent nested view (artifact plan). On disk these metadata_* keys are flattened to satisfy exp_sel_data_out.
